name: Sheep Laurel
summary: Low-stature (2–3 ft) evergreen ericaceous shrub for acid openings, regenerating clearcuts, and rocky barrens. Native to the eastern third of PA — the Pocono region is core habitat. Toxic to deer (a feature — establishes where almost nothing else can).
notes: Mid-June bloom (deep pink saucer flowers) provides bumblebee and azalea-specialist bee forage. Smaller and less light-blocking than mountain laurel — doesn't dominate the understory. Evergreen winter structure. Honest acid-soil match. Source from Tripple Brook Farm or other specialty Northeast natives.

planting.aspect: Acid openings; rocky barrens; regenerating clearcuts
planting.moisture: Dry to moist; tolerates wet edges
planting.site_notes: No deer protection needed — sheep laurel is toxic to deer, which avoid it. Plant in colonies of 5–10. Excellent successor in areas where mountain laurel hasn't established. Will not thrive on neutral or calcareous soils.
